title: Capresso Infinity Plus Conical Burr Grinder, Black
description:
About this item
Commercial grade grinding via solid steel burrs & advanced cutting design
Innovative gear reduction motor creates less friction and heat, preserving maximum flavor and aroma
16 grind settings: extra fine for Turkish to very coarse for percolators and cold brew
See-through bean container holds 11-oz of coffee beans
Removable ground coffee container holds up to 4-oz of ground coffee
reviews_5s:
First of all I really like this product. One of my old employers had one of these and it was much quieter than the previous one the office had. The plastic feels premium as far as plastic goes. The grinder itself is dead simple to use especially if you are like me and only grind what you need. I normally only put 1 to 2 scoops of coffee beans in the grinder at a time but I have heard you can fill it up and use the grind timer to estimate the amount of grounds you want. The tray area where the coffee grounds drop does get a little messy from fine coffee bean grounds overtime. This is easy to clean with a damp paper towel. You can clean the grinder by removing the hoper but I have not had to do this yet since I bought it but it is serviceable which I do appreciate.

There are not many cons for this grinder considering its feature set and price point. Yes you could buy a nice hand powered grinder for less than this one. Yes there are cheaper electric grinders out there. Yes there are even nicer grinders with more features and a more premium feel to them out there. This specific grinder hits a lot of sweet spots for me like I mentioned above. You got something that is relatively quiet for a coffee bean grinder, simple to use, easy to clean, and reliable. If you appreciate the pros I mentioned above then I am sure you will also appreciate this grinder like I do.Capresso Infinity Plus
(Silver) Model 575 05

Received on 2/20/21 - using Daily
- Hopper & Grounds (plastic) Container fit perfectly into respective locations (good, parts quality control)
- Hopper easily rotates for grind setting and easily removable (& inserted) when following Instructions
- Grounds Container’s top & sides a perfect fit (good, parts quality control)
- Grinding begins immediately upon turning Timer (to desired setting)
- Mechanically, Timer works well; easy adaptation to its operation
- Post-grinding: grounds (for my measure) do not find their way out of the Container, but are wholly captured inside (good, parts quality control)
- Static inside Grounds Container, after grinding, can be mitigated when removing (Hint: tap Container’s side immediately after slowly withdrawing)
- Upper & Lower Burrs: appear well made, but daily cleaning will be required (as with all burr grinders) for long term, consistent grinding
- ** Soft Collar (on Upper Burr) does not appear purchasable as a replacement part (it is not shown at Capresso’s web site & no mention in User Instructions)
- Soft Collar, which is to be in place (guides beans to Lower Burr chamber), simply slips around (using tension) the top-edge of Upper Burr; vulnerable to tearing or stretching (if badly or continually removed)
- softness of this Collar is no doubt, designed to gently guide beans
[if a Collar is critical to feeding beans to Lower Burrs & can not (ever) be purchased as a replaceable part, a different, collar design might have been better option, avoiding being rendered unusable. But that doesn't help those of us who now own this model Capresso, which has the Soft Collar.]
- Best to avoid any Soft Collar handling (as shown in Instructions) or removing

- Silver Model 575 05 weighs 8+ pounds
- Weight & Size a factor for cleaning Grind Chamber (removing Upper Burr; see Fig. 6 in User Guide); for some, possible issue since man-handling Grinder’s base (holding upside down) is unwieldy. (Hint: carefully use a small hand-vacuum, after brushing grinds loose, to suck out the remnants as well as any in the Feeder channel)

The Capresso Infinity Plus Model 575 05, is well made; manufacturing quality control exceeded expectation for this consumer model; easy operation; and grinding results have been very good & consistent and exceeded expectation

I like this Model grinder, I am glad to be using, and most importantly, I am happy with the grinding results.

UPDATED 3/31/21
- 4 (solid) stars: well made, good engineering quality-control & easy operability & very good grinding results

Rating Note: This Capresso 575 05 model could reasonably deserve a 5 star rating; however in order to gain the 5th star, long term reliability will be the benchmark, which can only be judged after a year's use.

- Cautionary Note: As a product sold for daily use, which requires daily cleaning (consistent with all burr grinders), & involves removing Upper Burr to clean both the Upper & Lower Burrs. This Soft Collar could easily be rendered unusable. [I was aware prior to my purchase.] Or as regular use continues, the soft material will age and may become less pliable (so, more prone to cracking).

Note to Retailers: Upper Burr’s Soft Collar should be purchasable as a replacement part
Note to Capresso: 1 Extra Soft Collar should be in the Box, when purchased New from any Retailer

3/31/21 Comment: I am now less concerned about vulnerability of the Soft Collar, given my cleaning routine does not include removing the Soft Collar.
- There are 2 prongs on the Upper Burrs element which enable grasping this element (to lift) without handling the Soft Collar.
- However, this does not change the possibility that some inadvertent action could occur which would damage the Soft Collar and necessitate purchasing a replacement. ...As result, I reiterate my Notes (above) to the Retailer & Capresso.

Updated 10/11/22 - changed rating to 5 Stars. Has fulfilled my hope for great grinding & reliability. Note: attribute latter to daily cleaning of grind port & grind burrs. Highly recommend.When my more-stylish conical burr grinder quit prematurely because its switch broke and I couldn't find a way to fix it, I replaced it with a Capresso grinder just like this one and despite inelegant appearance that machine performed sturdily for a good ten years before dying. The current specimen of the same model seems identical in most ways. Advantages over competitors include the conical burr, which avoids the excessive noise and dusty grind of a flat burr, and reliable mechanical fineness adjustment and timer. One thing that's worse: the static which is always an issue with these machines and causes coffee grounds to escape and make a mess. This one, grinding at the coarse end of medium, if opened directly sprays grounds around the counter and even the front of the machine. Not too hard to wipe up but still they get everywhere, little black flecks appearing in unfortunate places like the salt shaker for instance. Fortunately I can prevent that completely by putting a folded damp tea towel on the counter under the machine before turning it on.This is my first coffee grinder so I have nothing to compare it to. So I expected there to be something I would have to learn.

It will take a little playing around with the dial to understand what the numbers equate to the type of grind you get. So keep a coffee that you like on hand that is already ground up so that you can compare the grinds from this grinder to that of what you're familiar with.

Set the hopper to a grind setting you feel matches a coffee you like. Dial to #2 let it grind (this is so you don't grind too much). It starts grinding as soon as you move the dial.

Compare the grinds to what you have on hand. Repeat that if necessary till you get close to what you want. Then brew a cup or 2 to see if you like it. If not, keep trying till you get it.

Once you get it dialed in, you'll be able to grind a consistent result all the time.

Just be sure to make note of the grind setting of the hopper. Because when you remove the hopper for cleaning, this will remove the setting. So take a quick pic of it with your phone or mark it on a sticky note and attach that somewhere visible (on the fridge maybe?)

All in all, so far it's a great grinder. I would think it's way more quiet than a blade grinder since the beans aren't flying about in a container being beaten by a blade. They're simply being pulverized by the conical burr grinder. Which works pretty fast. As far as what it compares to in terms of noise, it's more quiet than a nutribullet when blending a shake.

Cleaning it is beyond easy. And there is plenty of room to fit a brush, and maybe a rag of some sort to help with it. Other grinders in the same price range seemed like they would be very difficult to clean when compared to this one.

I highly recommend this if it's your first grinder.This is my second Capreseo. The first one died after 12 years of daily use which may give you an idea of the sturdiness of Capreseo products.
I like the fact that it has industrial grade burr grinders, looks smart, and is very quiet, does an extremely great job of grinding beans into uniform grounds and most of all it's strictly mechanical.
There are no external electronic bells or whistles to fail. Very pleased with this purchase.Amazing! This Capresso grinder has been a game changer for my morning coffee. I used to use a blade grinder (gasp) and decided to advance to a burr grinder. I did extensive research into the best and most economical burr grinders and everything pointed to one in particular (best grind consistency, most grind settings) BUT it was out of the range of what I wanted to spend. This Capresso didn't come up as much in my research but a friend of mine recommended it to me. After viewing some comparisons it seemed like the best choice for me too. I opted for this Capresso Infinity Plus because I liked the look of the grinder (some of the other Capresso ones look like piano keys to me where the grind settings are). Now that I've had it for a couple months, it has been very easy to use and my coffee tastes amazing. I've used two different grind settings for pour-over and moka pot and both meet my expectations. The hopper holds a good amount of beans but is not too large (I usually fill about half-way full so that I can rotate in different coffee about every week). The timer dial is a great feature that I am really glad that it has (even though the research I read said this was not needed and weighing your ground coffee was better). There is a little bit of static and some grounds make it onto the counter but overall the mess is pretty minimal (way less than with my blade grinder). All in all this grinder has a good variety of grind settings for most methods of brewing, works great with very well designed features, and doesn't break the bank.Been a coffee roaster for decades. A good burr grinder is a must to get the best flavor out of coffee. Since coffee is my passion I have some of the best grinders on the market. But for friends and family that don’t have the money or just want an entry level grinder to explore amazing coffee. This is what I buy them. It can be limited on espresso. However, I have pulled some good shots on this thing. For French press, Moka Pot, Aeropress, Chemex, etc. This thing works greatI love this coffee grinder. We have had it a few years, and it works like we just bought it. It is easy to clean and as sharp as the first day we got it. My son is moving out and taking it with him. I will purchase it again!Gran selección me encantó su precio y su calidad. Su molido es perfecto me llenó las expectativas de calidad funcional.We bought this model to replace the same model grinder that was 8 years old. We expect to get the same service out of this replacement.
reviews_1s:
UPDATED with pictures of THREE (3) JUNKED Capresso by JURA
If any one wants them, they are going to be dropped off at the local transfer station (Town Dump) recycling shed (on Powisset St.)

BTW, two years ago, after the last of the failures, including paying the $100 for factory-rebuilt replacement, I gave up and have been very happy with the slightly less functional inexpensive grinder that is quieter, and perhaps because it is solid plastic does not have the same difficulty with static electricity causing build up of grinds on the container
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.8 years ago, my 50 year old Braun German-made grinder expired of old age. I purchased the Capresso 575 which for under two years was my dream coffee grinder. I've left multiple positive answers and great references for this machine since I bought it BUT NOT NOW (read on!) - first thing in the morning and late at night (I'm a retired firefighter/medic and got pushed into Public Health after retirement so starting in early March, 2020, have found myself working 16-20 hours per day/7 days a week for essentially no pay). The Capresso was so quiet and so reliable -- UNTIL IT WAS NOT! This morning, anticipating yet another day of increased infection rate, more citizen issues, and the need for my morning coffee at 5:48 a.m., I turned the time wheel and no power. The receptacle was not the problem. The grinder motor simply would not even operate. The Juro Capresso provides no reference nor assistance. Searches for troubleshooting suggest this can happen but no reason given. The power cord has never even been extended or stretched. The hopper sits and spins perfectly. No troubleshoot idea applies. It simply DIED. The base is impenetrable because Juro used bolts that are either proprietary or are non-removable. Answer IT Breaks, you buy again seems to be their answer. Can I recommend it now? NO - DO NOT BUY THIS BRAND IT IS UNRELIABLE!

ADDED 19-JAN-20
I do follow-up online purchases with recommendations and almost always the content is positive. Indeed, even for this purchase it was indeed very positive, as were the subsequent postings in response to questions of prospective purchasers. That all changed this weekend.

My days since early March have started anywhere from 5:30 a.m. to 6:15 a.m., and frequently last past midnight. I've typically run through 3 if not 4 of these 18 ounce mugs filled with brewed coffee (French Roast dark), and if I am lucky my spouse or my administrative assistant (depending on whether working from home office or municipal office) push me to have something to eat usually by 3 p.m. or later. This was January 11th at 4:08 p.m. picture with mug ;even while getting a bite, it is no less than two monitors open; office would show 4 monitors & 3 computers. The last thing I needed was my coffee grinder of less than 2 years age dying and leaving me without those 3 to 4 mugs to keep me going.I have an older model of this grinder that’s still going strong after 5 years. I bought this newer version 13 months ago. It has worked beautifully until 2 weeks ago when the timer knob broke and it stays on permanently unless you unplug it. I reached out to Capresso’s service department and they said it’s past the 12 month warranty so I’m out of luck. For a $186 dollar grinder, I would think they would stand behind their product more and be willing to replace it because it is clearly a defect. They offered me a discount on a new one at $105. I got this on Prime days so I paid $99. So the companies response is to pay more and not stand behind their product. Why in the world would I pay more for a product that will likely break in 13 months again and then I’m out $200 on their crummy product. Now I will be working with Amazing in hopes they are willing to work with me on a solution.
So this product would have had 5 stars but since it breaks quickly and not backed by the manufacturer and it costs a lot, they now get 1 star. I don’t recommend this product unless you have money to throw away.There is so much static buildup that when you empty the holding container, coffee grounds get all over the grinder. Horrible customer support. The. Infinity Ground Coffee Container cracked and after 6 weeks of waiting for the part I am told they don’t know when I can get itWhat I dislike about this product is very fragile after four months the machine is not working. This is the second Capresso I brought within the past 12 months . This product is inferior and I deserve a refund on at least one machine.
Thank-YouThis grinder is perfect for espresso for terrible for French Press as all of the settings grin the coffee way too fine. I am a French Press drinker and this grinder does NOT work for this application.It worked well two times. Then I just fit the transparent hopper on to the base. It just won’t align based on the two red lines. Very dissatisfied with purchase. Returning it.Big flaw in design. Luckily it happened to me in the first week of ownership. If you put the Burr in upside down, it drops in quite easily, it gets stuck and cannot be removed.

As for the grinding it was okay. The course sizes never really got very coarse. It was quiet enough, and not much static cleanuo.

But then again of course it doesn't really matter since it broke in the first week.

I should also add that I after returning this one I purchased the Oxo Conical Burr Coffee Grinder. And so far it's looking great there is a difference between all the grinds it's quiet enough hardly any static and the Oxo burr cannot be put back in upside down and it cleans easily too.This product does a good job of grinding the beans but the mess it makes is not really worth it. The unit builds up a static charge in the plastic container such that when the container is removed the chaf from the beans flys everywhere- and clings to the container and the entire unit. What a very poor design. I know there must be other conical grinders that do as good a job of grinding but aren't so messy.After reading through and following instruction guide, the grinder got clogged, then stopped.
Cleaned, restarted then stoped cold.I was initially impressed. The grind was consistent. I use it for drip coffee. 6mos in the grind consistency changed. Coffee tasted horrible. I noticed the area where the bean hopper slides into wasn't aligned correctly. Was able to move it back somewhat but it doesn't stay in place when you turn the hopper back. I always aligned the dots before turning the hopper. I clean it after every use. It has never been dropped. Very disappointed. only had it now 7 mos and it is useless. In search of a new one as store bought grind isn't consistent and my Moccamaster requires fresh ground. Uggg!
